inline_comments.message: Hi Sourabh, I am afraid you are mocking too much now and the is test is not testing what it was intended to test anymore. We'll discuss in te morning
As we discussed I used Spy or real object to invoke actual methods with sample data defined into unit test.

you can add some more detail like
- introduced JsonObjectMapper (wapper) as Spring component

Check copyright of ALL modified files. this should now become
Copyright (C) 2021-2022 Nordix Foundation

(it's a pain, I know...)
NB (non-blocking) legacy issue: this could be extracted out to local variabel as it is used on line 60 as well and it has gone a bit long no for inline....

NNB: I wonder if these object are really used default mock behavior is for all methods to return null...
Do these test fail if these are null? If need a 'Stub' is slight more lightweight and would be better if not really used
see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/24413184/difference-between-mock-stub-spy-in-spock-test-framework#:~:text=A%20stub%20is%20concerned%20with,is%20simpler%20than%20a%20stub.

open  to discussion (with team) but I would prefer slight short methodnames; the class is already called JsonObjectMapper so you can see names in teh context of the class name. Some suggestions (deliberately with classname and argument)

1) JsonObjectMapper.asJson(object)
2) JsonObjectMapper.asJsonString(object)
3) JsonObjectMapper.toJson(object)
4) JsonObjectMapper.toJsonString(object)

I think I prefer option 2....

can it convert any Object? or maybe JsonObjects ?! (should be clear for Groovy Spec)
Do we actually use this anywhere?

like above shorter  name suggestion

1) convert
2) convertTo
3) convertToType

like above shorter  name suggestion

1) convertString
2) convertJsonString
3) convertStringTo 
etc.

align description with code like so:

        then: 'the result is a valid json string (can be parsed)'
            def contentMap = new JsonSlurper().parseText(content)
         and: 'the parsed content is as expected'
            assert contentMap.'test:bookstore'.'bookstore-name' == 'Chapters'

be more specific like so:
(also combined with next test, conveting to an 'Object' is not  a great testcase and doe snot a test-coverage of our code really)

        when: 'converted into a Map'
            def result = jsonObjectMapper.convertFromValueToValueType(contentMap, Map);
        then: 'the result is a mapped into class of type Map'
            assert result instanceof Map
        and: 'the map contains the expected key'
            assert result.containsKey('test:bookstore')
    }
you can add this to the previous test, not need for a separate test...
replace with smaller example to make it easier to see what is wrong
Not sure why this was removed. Seems to be causing failure in CSIT tests. See build log: https://jenkins.onap.org/job/cps-review-verification-maven-master/1144/console
Copyright across all edited files needs to be updated for nordix foundation to 2021-2022
Non static imports should be grouped with other non static imports below the static ones.
These should be removed if they are not being used

I thought we moved away of from this method of injecting dependencies through tags and autowiring?

I remember as part of this commit adding constructors manually.
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/122418/11/cps-ncmp-service/src/main/java/org/onap/cps/ncmp/api/impl/NetworkCmProxyDataServiceImpl.java#60
Read comment on previous constructor tag
Not being used as part of this test class
Group with other non static imports
Read other comments related to this

What is the difference between readValue and convertValue within Object Mapper?

Should the method name be different to reflect this?
Yes it need as used into controller.
we need Stub() here as it is used by jsonObjectMapper.asJsonString(requestBody) at multiple places.

It is used as parameter 1 of constructor in org.onap.cps.ncmp.api.impl.operations.DmiDataOperations and further at line no 235 jsonObjectMapper.convertJsonString(fragmentEntity.getAttributes(), Map.class); of CpsDataPersistenceServiceImpl. Without this bean it throws "NoSuchBeanDefinitionException".

This bean is used into Parameter 1 of constructor in org.onap.cps.rest.controller.DataRestController and further used at line no 169 jsonObjectMapper.asJsonString(dataNode.getLeaves()); of CpsDataPersistenceServiceImpl class.

This bean is used into Parameter 1 of constructor in org.onap.cps.rest.controller.QueryRestController and further used at line no 235 jsonObjectMapper.convertJsonString(fragmentEntity.getAttributes(), Map.class); of CpsDataPersistenceServiceImpl class.
Used 'Stub' as it's more lightweight and would be better if not really used.

This object is being used by it's all child class CpsAdminPersistenceServiceSpec, CpsDataPersistenceQueryDataNodeSpec, CpsDataPersistenceServiceIntegrationSpec, CpsModulePersistenceServiceIntegrationSpec etc.
It converts only compatible json object.
convertValue takes json Object as param where as readValue takes json string to convert into value type.

I cant remmer a (team) decision on this Dylan. I think it is OK using an allArgs constructor makes it easier to test using plain-old-Java with need for SpringBoot testing... If needed we can discuss this at standup but II approve as it is now. We can always revert later if needed but it would have wider implications...

Non-Blocking, Legacy issue: apparently we can use Stub() in most test unless we are interested in checking interactions ie. assert "1 * someMock.someMethod()"

I wont comment on any if this but going forward I think we should try to use stubs over mocks...
NB: this is a new class, so it shoudl just have been 2022: Copyright (C) 2022 Nordix Foundation
No problem. I thought it was done for security reasons, but that could just have been the Autowired tag :)
